---
title: "Avian_final_code"
author: "Stephanie Waller"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
---

```{r set working directory}
getwd()

setwd("/Users/walst32p/Desktop/Avian/R_code")
```

```{r Oral vs Cloacal Swabs}
#1. Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

#2. Read in your data
df <- read.csv("RPM_Order.csv", header = TRUE)


#3. Pivot from wide to long format
df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = Adenoviridae:Tobaniviridae,  # adjust if other viral families are included
    names_to = "Viral_Family",
    values_to = "RPM"
  )

df_grouped <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Sample_Type, Viral_Family) %>%
  summarise(Total_RPM = sum(RPM), .groups = "drop")

#4. Plot: Stacked bar plot
ggplot(df_grouped, aes(x = Sample_Type, y = Total_RPM, fill = Viral_Family)) +
  geom_bar(position="fill", stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "Sample Type", y = "Total Reads Per Million", fill = "Viral Family") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5))




# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Step 1: Read the data
df <- read.csv("RPM_Order.csv", header = TRUE)

# Step 2: Pivot to long format (viral families into rows)
df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = Adenoviridae:Tobaniviridae,
    names_to = "Viral_Family",
    values_to = "RPM"
  )

# Step 3: Summarise RPM per sample type and viral family
df_summary <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Sample_Type, Viral_Family) %>%
  summarise(Total_RPM = sum(RPM), .groups = "drop")

# Step 4: Plot pie chart for each sample type
ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = "", y = Total_RPM, fill = Viral_Family)) +
  geom_bar(position="fill", stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar("y") +
  facet_wrap(~ Sample_Type) +
  labs(title = "Viral Family Composition by Swab Type",
       y = "", x = "", fill = "Viral Family") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

```

```{r Number of bird species and viral familys in each order}
# Load libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)

# Step 1: Read in the data
df <- read.csv("RPM_Order.csv", header = TRUE)

# Step 2a: Count number of unique bird species per order
species_count <- df %>%
  distinct(Bird_order, Bird_species) %>%
  count(Bird_order, name = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Category = "Bird species")

# Step 2b: Count number of viral families detected (value > 0) per order
viral_families <- df %>%
  select(Bird_order, Adenoviridae:Tobaniviridae) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Bird_order, names_to = "Viral_Family", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  distinct(Bird_order, Viral_Family) %>%
  count(Bird_order, name = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Category = "Viral families")

# Step 3: Combine both counts
combined_counts <- bind_rows(species_count, viral_families)

# Step 4: Set custom order of Bird_order
combined_counts$Bird_order <- factor(combined_counts$Bird_order,
                                     levels = c("Charadriiformes", "Procellariiformes",
                                                "Sphenisciformes", "Anseriformes"))

# Step 5: Plot
ggplot(combined_counts, aes(x = Bird_order, y = Value, fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Value),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  labs(x = "Bird Order", y = "Count", fill = "",
       title = "Bird Species and Viral Families per Bird Order") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


```

```{r Normalised viral abundance by General_Location Bird Order and Bird Species}
# Load libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Step 1: Load normalised abundance data
normalised_raw <- read.csv("Normalised.csv", header = TRUE, row.names = 1)

# Step 2: Transpose so families are rows, libraries are columns
normalised <- t(normalised_raw)

# Step 3: Convert to data frame
normalised_df <- as.data.frame(normalised)

# Step 4: Add viral family as a column
normalised_df$family <- rownames(normalised_df)

# Step 5: Reshape to long format
melted <- melt(normalised_df, id.vars = "family", variable.name = "library", value.name = "abundance")

# Step 6: Load library metadata (e.g. from RPM_Order.csv)
metadata <- read.csv("RPM_Order.csv", header = TRUE)

# Step 7: Merge metadata with melted data
melted$library <- as.character(melted$library)
metadata$Bird_lib <- as.character(metadata$Bird_lib)

merged <- left_join(melted, metadata, by = c("library" = "Bird_lib"))

# Step 8: Create unique label using General_Location, Bird_order, Bird_species, and library ID
merged <- merged %>%
  mutate(library_label = paste(General_Location, Bird_order, Bird_species, library, sep = " | "))

# Step 9: Order factor levels by General_Location > Bird_order > Bird_species
merged$library_label <- factor(merged$library_label,
                               levels = merged %>%
                                 distinct(library, library_label, General_Location, Bird_order, Bird_species) %>%
                                 arrange(General_Location, Bird_order, Bird_species) %>%
                                 pull(library_label))

# Step 10: Plot
ggplot(merged, aes(x = library_label, y = family, fill = abundance)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "blue", trans = "log10") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Library (Grouped by Region, Order, and Species)", y = "Viral Family", fill = "Normalised Abundance") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8))

write.csv(merged, "merged_data_heatmap.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```


```{r Network analysis by Bird Species}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(network)
library(igraph)
library(sna)
library(devtools)
library(ggnet)
library(ergm)
library(intergraph)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggbipart)
library(tibble)

# Read in your data
data_birds <- read.csv("RPM_Order.csv", header = TRUE)

# Optional: clean species names (e.g., replace spaces with underscores)
data_birds$Bird_species <- gsub(" ", "_", data_birds$Bird_species)

# Step 1: Aggregate by bird species (mean RPM per species)
library(dplyr)
bird_virus_matrix <- data_birds %>%
  group_by(Bird_species) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("Adenoviridae"):last_col(), sum)) %>%
  column_to_rownames("Bird_species")

# Step 2: Convert to matrix
bird_virus_matrix <- as.matrix(bird_virus_matrix)

# Step 3: Create bipartite network objects
bird_net <- bip_init_network(bird_virus_matrix)
bird_ig  <- bip_init_igraph(bird_virus_matrix)

# Step 4: Plot with ggbipart
bird_bipart_plot <- bip_ggnet(bird_net, bird_virus_matrix,
                              label = TRUE)

# Display the plot
bird_bipart_plot

bird_virus_binary <- ifelse(bird_virus_matrix > 0, 1, 0)
library(bipartite)
networklevel(bird_virus_binary, index=c("nestedness", "connectance", "H2"))
mod <- computeModules(bird_virus_binary)
summary(mod)


```

```{r Number of novel viral species vs non novel species}
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Read the CSV
novel_data <- read_csv("Novel_Not_Novel.csv")

# Clean column names (replace spaces with underscores)
colnames(novel_data) <- gsub(" ", "_", colnames(novel_data))

# Pivot to long format
novel_long <- pivot_longer(novel_data, cols = c(Novel, Not_novel),
                           names_to = "Novelty", values_to = "Count")

# Plot
ggplot(novel_long, aes(x = Viral_family, y = Count, fill = Novelty)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  labs(title = "Counts of Novel vs Not Novel Viruses by Viral Family",
       x = "Viral Family",
       y = "Count") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Novel" = "#2C7BB6", "Not_novel" = "#D7191C")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


```


```{r Network analysis of viral sharing by bird species}
#####Only by bird species####

# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(network)
library(igraph)
library(sna)
library(devtools)
library(ggnet)
library(ergm)
library(intergraph)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggbipart)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Read in your data
data_birds_vs <- read.csv("viral_sharingV2.csv", header = TRUE)

# Clean species names
data_birds_vs$bird_species <- gsub(" ", "_", data_birds_vs$bird_species)

# Step 1: Summarise RPM per bird species × virus
bird_virus_matrix <- data_birds_vs %>%
  group_by(bird_species, virus) %>%
  summarise(RPM = sum(RPM, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = virus, values_from = RPM, values_fill = 0) %>%
  column_to_rownames("bird_species")

# Step 2: Convert to matrix
bird_virus_matrix <- as.matrix(bird_virus_matrix)

# Step 3: Create bipartite network
bird_net <- bip_init_network(bird_virus_matrix)
bird_ig  <- bip_init_igraph(bird_virus_matrix)

# Step 4: Plot with ggbipart (edge width reflects RPM by default)
bird_bipart_plot <- bip_ggnet(
  bird_net,
  bird_virus_matrix,
  label = TRUE
)

# Show plot
bird_bipart_plot


```



```{r NMDS on virome composition by Bird Order without 33}
# Load required packagesq
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Step 1: Load the data
df <- read_csv("RPM_Order.csv")

# Step 2: Remove library 33
df <- df %>% filter(Bird_lib != 33)

# Step 3: Subset the viral abundance data
viral_data <- df %>% select(Adenoviridae:Tobaniviridae)

# Step 4: Filter out samples with all zeros
non_zero_rows <- rowSums(viral_data) > 0
df_filtered <- df[non_zero_rows, ]
viral_data_filtered <- viral_data[non_zero_rows, ]

# Step 5: Create Bray-Curtis distance matrix
bray_dist <- vegdist(viral_data_filtered, method = "bray")

# Step 6: Run NMDS
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
nmds <- metaMDS(viral_data_filtered, distance = "bray", k = 2)

# Step 7: Plot stress plot (optional)
stressplot(nmds)

# Step 8: Combine NMDS points with metadata
mds_points <- as.data.frame(nmds$points)
mds_combined <- cbind(mds_points, Bird_order = df_filtered$Bird_order)

# Step 9: NMDS plot coloured by bird order
ggplot(mds_combined, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2, color = Bird_order)) +
  geom_jitter(size = 3, width = 0.1, height = 0.1) +  # Add jitter
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  labs(title = "NMDS of Viral Communities by Bird Order",
       x = "NMDS1", y = "NMDS2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank())

# Step 10: PERMANOVA testing effect of bird order on beta diversity
set.seed(123)
adonis_result <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ Bird_order, data = df_filtered, permutations = 999)

# Step 11: Print result
print(adonis_result)

#          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
#Model      3    3.527 0.05431 2.6417  0.001 ***

```


```{r NMDS on virome composition by Scavenging without 33}
# Load required packages
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Step 1: Load the data
df <- read_csv("RPM_Order.csv")

# Step 2: Remove library 33
df <- df %>% filter(Bird_lib != 33)

# Step 3: Subset the viral abundance data
viral_data <- df %>% select(Adenoviridae:Tobaniviridae)

# Step 4: Filter out samples with all zeros
non_zero_rows <- rowSums(viral_data) > 0
df_filtered <- df[non_zero_rows, ]
viral_data_filtered <- viral_data[non_zero_rows, ]

# Step 5: Create Bray-Curtis distance matrix
bray_dist <- vegdist(viral_data_filtered, method = "bray")

# Step 6: Run NMDS
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
nmds <- metaMDS(viral_data_filtered, distance = "bray", k = 2)

# Step 7: Plot stress plot (optional)
stressplot(nmds)

# Step 8: Combine NMDS points with metadata
mds_points <- as.data.frame(nmds$points)
mds_combined <- cbind(mds_points, Sacvening_status = df_filtered$Sacvening_status)

# Step 9: NMDS plot coloured by General Location
ggplot(mds_combined, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2, color = Sacvening_status)) +
  geom_jitter(size = 3, width = 0.1, height = 0.1) +  # Adds jitter
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  labs(title = "NMDS of Viral Communities by Sacvening_status",
       x = "NMDS1", y = "NMDS2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank())

# Step 10: PERMANOVA testing effect of General_Location on beta diversity
set.seed(123)
adonis_result <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ Sacvening_status, data = df_filtered, permutations = 999)

# Step 11: Print result
print(adonis_result)
#          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)   
#Model      2    4.101 0.06315 4.6849  0.001 ***



set.seed(123)
adonis2(bray_dist ~ Bird_order + General_Location + Sample_Type + Sacvening_status + Migration, 
        data = df_filtered, method = "bray", by = "margin", permutations = 999)

print(adonis_result)

betadisper_result <- betadisper(bray_dist, df_filtered$Sacvening_status)
anova(betadisper_result)

plot(betadisper_result)      # centroids + hulls
boxplot(betadisper_result)   # distance to centroid per group

permutest(betadisper_result, pairwise = TRUE)

boxplot(betadisper_result, main = "Distance to Centroid by Scavenging Status",
        ylab = "Distance to group centroid")

```

```{r NMDS Scavenging, further analysis without 33}
library(indicspecies)

# community data matrix (e.g., abundance or presence/absence)
comm <- viral_data_filtered

# grouping factor
group <- df_filtered$Sacvening_status

# Run the indicator species analysis (default is IndVal.g, which handles abundance data)
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
indval_result <- multipatt(comm, group, control = how(nperm=999))

summary(indval_result)

# Extract the viral families identified as indicators
indicator_taxa <- c("Adenoviridae", "Astroviridae", "Coronaviridae", "Papillomaviridae")

# Subset the viral abundance data for these taxa
indicator_data <- viral_data_filtered[, indicator_taxa]

# Combine with scavenging status metadata
plot_data <- cbind(df_filtered["Sacvening_status"], indicator_data)

# Optional: rename the metadata column if needed
colnames(plot_data)[1] <- "Scavenging_status"

library(tidyr)

plot_data_long <- plot_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(indicator_taxa),
    names_to = "Viral_Family",
    values_to = "Abundance"
  )

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(plot_data_long, aes(x = Scavenging_status, y = Abundance, fill = Scavenging_status)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +   # boxplots without outliers shown twice
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) + # jittered points
  facet_wrap(~ Viral_Family, scales = "free_y") +   # separate panels for each viral family
  labs(title = "Indicator Viral Families Abundance by Scavenging Status",
       x = "Scavenging Status",
       y = "Viral Abundance (RPM or Counts)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")


library(ggpubr)

# Define pairwise comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(
  c("No", "Occasionally"),
  c("No", "Yes"),
  c("Occasionally", "Yes")
)

ggplot(plot_data_long, aes(x = Scavenging_status, y = Abundance, fill = Scavenging_status)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Viral_Family, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Indicator Viral Families Abundance by Scavenging Status",
       x = "Scavenging Status",
       y = "Viral Abundance (RPM or Counts)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, 
                     method = "wilcox.test",   # non-parametric test
                     label = "p.signif",       # show significance levels (*, **, ***)
                     hide.ns = TRUE)           # hide non-significant comparisons


```

```{r Shannon, Bird_Order}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)  # For diversity() function

# Read in your data
data_birds <- read.csv("RPM_Order.csv", header = TRUE)

# Step 1: Identify viral family columns
viral_cols <- which(names(data_birds) %in% c("Adenoviridae", "Anelloviridae", "Astroviridae", 
                                             "Caliciviridae", "Coronaviridae", "Flaviviridae",
                                             "Hepadnaviridae", "Orthoherpesviridae", "Orthomyxoviridae", 
                                             "Papillomaviridae", "Paramyxoviridae", "Picornaviridae", 
                                             "Pneumoviridae", "Polyomaviridae", "Sedoreoviridae", 
                                             "Spinareoviridae", "Tobaniviridae"))

# Step 2: Calculate Shannon index per sample
# vegan::diversity requires non-negative abundance values
data_birds$shannon <- diversity(data_birds[, viral_cols], index = "shannon")

# Step 3: Summarize Shannon index by Bird_order
order_summary <- data_birds %>%
  group_by(Bird_order) %>%
  summarise(mean_shannon = mean(shannon),
            sd_shannon = sd(shannon),
            n = n(),
            se_shannon = sd_shannon / sqrt(n))

# View summary table
print(order_summary)

# Step 4: Plot Shannon index per Bird_order
ggplot(data_birds, aes(x = Bird_order, y = shannon)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Shannon Diversity Index per Bird Order",
       y = "Shannon Diversity (H')",
       x = "Bird Order") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Step 5: Kruskal-Wallis test for Shannon index
kruskal.test(shannon ~ Bird_order, data = data_birds)
#Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 5.1472, df = 3, p-value = 0.1613

```

```{r Shannon, Scavenging}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)     # For diversity() function
library(FSA)       # For dunnTest()
library(ggpubr)    # For stat_pvalue_manual()

# Read in your data
data_birds <- read.csv("RPM_Order.csv", header = TRUE)

# Identify viral family columns
viral_cols <- which(names(data_birds) %in% c(
  "Adenoviridae", "Anelloviridae", "Astroviridae", 
  "Caliciviridae", "Coronaviridae", "Flaviviridae",
  "Hepadnaviridae", "Orthoherpesviridae", "Orthomyxoviridae", 
  "Papillomaviridae", "Paramyxoviridae", "Picornaviridae", 
  "Pneumoviridae", "Polyomaviridae", "Sedoreoviridae", 
  "Spinareoviridae", "Tobaniviridae"))

# Calculate Shannon index
data_birds$shannon <- diversity(data_birds[, viral_cols], index = "shannon")

# Summary stats
sacvening_summary <- data_birds %>%
  group_by(Sacvening_status) %>%
  summarise(mean_shannon = mean(shannon),
            sd_shannon = sd(shannon),
            n = n(),
            se_shannon = sd_shannon / sqrt(n))

# Kruskal-Wallis test
kruskal_result <- kruskal.test(shannon ~ Sacvening_status, data = data_birds)
print(kruskal_result)

# Dunn's post hoc test (Benjamini-Hochberg correction)
dunn_result <- dunnTest(shannon ~ Sacvening_status, data = data_birds, method = "bh")
dunn_df <- dunn_result$res

# Filter significant comparisons (adjusted p < 0.05)
sig_dunn <- dunn_df %>% 
  filter(P.adj < 0.05) %>%
  mutate(group1 = gsub(" -.*", "", Comparison),
         group2 = gsub(".*- ", "", Comparison),
         y.position = max(data_birds$shannon, na.rm = TRUE) + seq(0.05, 0.2, length.out = n())) %>%
  select(group1, group2, p.adj = P.adj, y.position)

# Create the plot with annotations
ggplot(data_birds, aes(x = Sacvening_status, y = shannon)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightcoral", color = "black", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Shannon Diversity Index by Sacvening Status",
       y = "Shannon Diversity (H')",
       x = "Sacvening Status") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(sig_dunn, label = "p.adj", tip.length = 0.01)

dunn_result <- dunnTest(shannon ~ Sacvening_status, data = data_birds, method = "bh") 
dunn_result

```


```{r NMDS on virome composition by Scavenging for Non-avian viruses}
# Load required packages
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Step 1: Load the data
df <- read_csv("NotAvianRPM.csv")

# Step 3: Subset the viral abundance data
viral_data <- df %>% select(Ackermannviridae:Unclassified)

# Step 4: Filter out samples with all zeros
non_zero_rows <- rowSums(viral_data) > 0
df_filtered <- df[non_zero_rows, ]
viral_data_filtered <- viral_data[non_zero_rows, ]

# Step 5: Create Bray-Curtis distance matrix
bray_dist <- vegdist(viral_data_filtered, method = "bray")

# Step 6: Run NMDS
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
nmds <- metaMDS(viral_data_filtered, distance = "bray", k = 2)

# Step 7: Plot stress plot (optional)
stressplot(nmds)

# Step 8: Combine NMDS points with metadata
mds_points <- as.data.frame(nmds$points)
mds_combined <- cbind(mds_points, Sacvening_status = df_filtered$Sacvening_status)

# Step 9: NMDS plot coloured by General Location
ggplot(mds_combined, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2, color = Sacvening_status)) +
  geom_jitter(size = 3, width = 0.1, height = 0.1) +  # Adds jitter
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  labs(title = "NMDS of Viral Communities by Sacvening_status",
       x = "NMDS1", y = "NMDS2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank())

# Step 10: PERMANOVA testing effect of General_Location on beta diversity
set.seed(123)
adonis_result <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ Sacvening_status, data = df_filtered, permutations = 999)

# Step 11: Print result
print(adonis_result)

#Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
#Permutation: free
#Number of permutations: 999

#adonis2(formula = bray_dist ~ Sacvening_status, data = df_filtered, permutations = 999)
#          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
#Model      2    2.264 0.03305 3.4178  0.001 ***

#There is a statistically significant difference in virome composition between scavenging categories, but the effect is small (only 3.3% of variation explained)

set.seed(123)
adonis2(bray_dist ~ Bird_order + General_Location + Sample_Type + Sacvening_status + Migration, 
        data = df_filtered, method = "bray", by = "margin", permutations = 999)

print(adonis_result)

#adonis2(formula = bray_dist ~ Bird_order + General_Location + Sample_Type + Sacvening_status + Migration, data = df_filtered, permutations = 999, method = "bray", by = "margin")
#                  Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
#Bird_order         3    1.913 0.02792 2.1386  0.001 ***
#General_Location   3    1.897 0.02769 2.1210  0.002 ** 
#Sample_Type        1    2.893 0.04223 9.7049  0.001 ***
#Sacvening_status   2    1.387 0.02025 2.3262  0.008 ** 
#Migration          2    0.874 0.01276 1.4659  0.090 .  
#Residual         191   56.937 0.83118                  
#Total            202   68.501 1.00000



```

```{r Shannon, Scavenging non-avian viruses}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)     # For diversity() function
library(FSA)       # For dunnTest()
library(ggpubr)    # For stat_pvalue_manual()

# Read in your data
data_birds <- read.csv("NotAvianRPM.csv", header = TRUE)

# Identify viral family columns
viral_cols <- which(names(data_birds) == "Ackermannviridae") :
              which(names(data_birds) == "Unclassified")


# Calculate Shannon index
data_birds$shannon <- diversity(data_birds[, viral_cols], index = "shannon")

# Summary stats
sacvening_summary <- data_birds %>%
  group_by(Sacvening_status) %>%
  summarise(mean_shannon = mean(shannon),
            sd_shannon = sd(shannon),
            n = n(),
            se_shannon = sd_shannon / sqrt(n))

# Kruskal-Wallis test
kruskal_result <- kruskal.test(shannon ~ Sacvening_status, data = data_birds)
print(kruskal_result)

# Dunn's post hoc test (Benjamini-Hochberg correction)
dunn_result <- dunnTest(shannon ~ Sacvening_status, data = data_birds, method = "bh")
dunn_df <- dunn_result$res

# Filter significant comparisons (adjusted p < 0.05)
sig_dunn <- dunn_df %>% 
  filter(P.adj < 0.05) %>%
  mutate(group1 = gsub(" -.*", "", Comparison),
         group2 = gsub(".*- ", "", Comparison),
         y.position = max(data_birds$shannon, na.rm = TRUE) + seq(0.05, 0.2, length.out = n())) %>%
  select(group1, group2, p.adj = P.adj, y.position)

# Create the plot with annotations
ggplot(data_birds, aes(x = Sacvening_status, y = shannon)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightcoral", color = "black", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Shannon Diversity Index by Sacvening Status",
       y = "Shannon Diversity (H')",
       x = "Sacvening Status") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(sig_dunn, label = "p.adj", tip.length = 0.01)

dunn_result <- dunnTest(shannon ~ Sacvening_status, data = data_birds, method = "bh") 
dunn_result

```
